name: evil-winrm
summary:
Evil-WinRM [![Version-shield]](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Hackplayers/evil-winrm/master/evil-winrm.rb) [![Ruby2.3-shield]](https://www.ruby-lang.org/en/news/2015/12/25/ruby-2-3-0-released/) [![Gem-Version]](https://rubygems.org/gems/evil-winrm) [![License-shield]](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Hackplayers/evil-winrm/master/LICENSE) [![Docker-shield]](https://hub.docker.com/r/oscarakaelvis/evil-winrm/)
The ultimate WinRM shell for hacking/pentesting

documentation.readme: Description & Purpose This shell is the ultimate WinRM shell for hacking/pentesting.
usage_sections: Features: - Compatible to Linux and Windows client systems - Load in memory Powershell scripts - Load in memory dll files bypassing some AVs - Load in memory C# (C Sharp) assemblies bypassing some AVs - Load x64 payloads generated with awesome [donut] technique - Dynamic AMSI Bypass to avo

arguments.name: -a
arguments.description: Specify connection user-agent (default Microsoft WinRM Client)

arguments.name: -Binary
arguments.description: allows .Net assemblies to be executed in memory. The name can be auto-completed using tab key. Arguments for the exe file can be passed comma separated. Example: `Invoke-Binary /op

arguments.name: -c
arguments.description: Local path to public key certificate

arguments.name: --ccache
arguments.description: Path to Kerberos ticket file (ccache or kirbi format, auto-detected)

arguments.name: -i
arguments.description: Remote host IP or hostname. FQDN for Kerberos auth (required)

arguments.name: --ip
arguments.description: Remote host IP or hostname. FQDN for Kerberos auth (required)

arguments.name: -K
arguments.description: Path to Kerberos ticket file (ccache or kirbi format, auto-detected)

arguments.name: -k
arguments.description: Local path to private key certificate

arguments.name: -Loader
arguments.description: allows loading dll libraries in memory, it is equivalent to: `[Reflection.Assembly]::Load([IO.File]::ReadAllBytes("pwn.dll"))`

arguments.name: --priv-key
arguments.description: Local path to private key certificate

arguments.name: --pub-key
arguments.description: Local path to public key certificate

arguments.name: -r
arguments.description: Kerberos auth, it has to be set also in /etc/krb5.conf file using this format -> CONTOSO.COM = { kdc = fooserver.contoso.com }

arguments.name: --realm
arguments.description: Kerberos auth, it has to be set also in /etc/krb5.conf file using this format -> CONTOSO.COM = { kdc = fooserver.contoso.com }

arguments.name: --spn
arguments.description: SPN prefix for Kerberos auth (default HTTP)

arguments.name: --user-agent
arguments.description: Specify connection user-agent (default Microsoft WinRM Client)

examples: Usage: evil-winrm -i IP -u USER [-s SCRIPTS_PATH] [-e EXES_PATH] [-P PORT] [-a USERAGENT] [-p PASS] [-H HASH] [-U URL] [-S] [-c PUBLIC_KEY_PATH ] [-k PRIVATE_KEY_PATH ] [-r REALM] [-K TICKET_FILE] [--spn SPN_PREFIX] [-l]
Relative paths are not allowed to use on download/upload. Use filenames on current directory or absolute path.
Let's suppose that you have in your Debian based system ruby 2.7.3:
